messages.author: SKitter_
messages.text: I’ve been kicking around an idea for MeshCore and wanted to see if anyone else thinks it’s useful. Basically a “Post Office” / Mailbox node for delayed DM delivery. Right now if I send a DM and the person isn’t reachable at that moment, it can just fail. Room Server doesn’t really solve this since it’s shared room history and a small rolling cache, not private per-user message storage. The idea would be something like this: you send a normal DM, MeshCore tries direct and then flood like it already does, and if delivery still isn’t confirmed, the same encrypted message gets stored at a Post Office node. Then later the recipient can query it, pull their messages, and ACK them so they get deleted. It would still be end-to-end encrypted, the Post Office just holds opaque payloads. The original message stays the same (same timestamp/identity), so it’s not creating a new message, just another delivery path. Duplicates are fine, losing messages isn’t. Important part is this would be a split setup: the radio is just the mesh-facing node, and a connected host (Pi/laptop/etc.) runs a daemon that handles storage, inboxes, TTL, etc. So it’s not just firmware, it would touch firmware, a host service, and the app UX. I’m not really in a position to build all of that myself, so I figured I’d throw it out here: does this sound useful? is anyone already working on something like this? would this belong upstream or more as a separate project? would anyone want to collaborate on it? I’ve got a more detailed spec written up if anyone’s interested.

Then later the recipient can query it, pull their messages, and ACK them so they get deleted. It would still be end-to-end encrypted, the Post Office just holds opaque payloads. The original message stays the same (same timestamp/identity), so it’s not creating a new message, just another delivery path. Duplicates are fine, losing messages isn’t. Important part is this would be a split setup: the radio is just the mesh-facing node, and a connected host (Pi/laptop/etc.) runs a daemon that handles storage, inboxes, TTL, etc. So it’s not just firmware, it would touch firmware, a host service, and the app UX. I’ve got a more detailed spec written up if anyone’s interested.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: SKitter_
messages.text: I’ve started tinkering with vibe-coding this. Not sure if I’ll be able to get it to work or not.

messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: Room server code brings you 90% of the way there without, just without the delete functionality (I think).
messages.content_html: Room server code brings you 90% of the way there without, just without the delete functionality (I think).
